_about: spck.dev site claims register — every factual claim on the site that is not data-rendered from coverage/agent-coverage JSON must be registered here with evidence and a review-by date, or removed. Bootstrapped by ops/site-content-audit-2026-07.md (Phase 0 of the 2026-07-09 IA redesign). The manifest block is the bindable public source for product counts that do NOT exist in coverage JSONs (which count MUST rows, not checks); the site-freshness gate regenerates these numbers from the engine and goes RED on drift.

claims.text: 193 kill-rate-validated checks, live coverage matrix, CLI + CI + web, multi-version support.
claims.evidence: conformance/coverage/coverage_gate.py copy-freshness gate counts MCheck( registrations across conformance/checks/merchant_checks*.py = 193 (verified 2026-07-09) and pins this exact phrase via regex; bind number to site_claims.json:$.manifest.merchant_checks

Merchant-side checks hero stat — gate-enforced by coverage_gate.py stat-num regex; NOT in coverage.json (its check fields count MUST rows 117/125/234); data-live bind to site_claims.json:$.manifest.merchant_checks

claims.text: Every check is proven to catch its own bug (kill-rate validated).
claims.evidence: conformance/selfcheck/mutation_killrate.py + conformance/selfcheck/validate_merchant_checks.py — run_suite gates 'merchant' and 'killrate' block release if any check fails to kill its injected defect

claims.text: every check is proven to catch the bug it’s for, so there are no false greens
claims.evidence: conformance/selfcheck/mutation_killrate.py + validate_merchant_checks.py (merchant lane) and conformance/agent/run_agent.py reference gate (agent lane): clean-pass AND kill required per check

'False greens' hero stat — kill-rate mechanics: a check that survives its targeted defect is flagged UNSAFE and blocks release (mutation_killrate.py; validate_merchant_checks.py; run_agent.py reference gate)

Agent-side checks hero stat — conformance/agent/agent_governance.py _agent_copy_freshness() enforces len(agent_checks.CHECKS)=42 (verified live import 2026-07-09); NOT in agent-coverage.json (its check=53 counts covered MUST rows); data-live bind to site_claims.json:$.manifest.agent_checks

claims.text: Every normative MUST across three spec versions is accounted for
claims.evidence: conformance/selfcheck/verify_register_completeness.py (denominator complete) + conformance/coverage/coverage_gate.py; every public/coverage.json row carries status check/exempt/gap; 'three' should render from coverage.json:$.spec_pins key count

claims.text: 193 checks, from the browser or your CI
claims.evidence: coverage_gate.py copy-freshness regex '(\d+)\+? checks, from the browser' pins this to the real MCheck count (193, verified 2026-07-09)

claims.text: regenerated with every change and enforced by CI (stale data or a coverage regression fails the build)
claims.evidence: conformance/coverage/coverage_gate.py freshness comparison + conformance/coverage/ratchet.json accounted-coverage floors

claims.text: Roadmap to 100%
claims.evidence: docs/ROADMAP.md (link target exists, verified 2026-07-09)

claims.text: is maintained by Google's UCP team at
claims.evidence: https://ucp.dev — About-block attribution; the only context where 'Google's UCP team' is allowed by the voice rules (mark block data-attribution)

claims.text: Google's Python test suite (targets v2026-01-23)
claims.evidence: https://github.com/Universal-Commerce-Protocol/ucp — official conformance suite; re-verify the targeted spec version at each review (external fact, can drift)

claims.text: Released April 9, 2026
claims.evidence: conformance/SOURCES.lock.json pin v2026-04-08 + https://github.com/Universal-Commerce-Protocol/ucp/releases; version id itself is LIVE-able via coverage.json:$.spec_pins

claims.text: Released January 23, 2026
claims.evidence: conformance/SOURCES.lock.json pin v2026-01-23 (commit_date 2026-01-23) + upstream releases page

claims.text: Released January 11, 2026
claims.evidence: conformance/SOURCES.lock.json pin v2026-01-11 + upstream releases page

claims.text: Anything it can't test is reported
claims.evidence: conformance/checks/merchant.py capability scoping + not-tested reporting; README.md 'reports only the checks it actually runs'

claims.text: every check is kill-rate tested — we inject the exact defect it should catch, and if it still passes, it's blocked from release
claims.evidence: conformance/selfcheck/mutation_killrate.py (incl. the deliberate noop-check canary proving the harness works)

claims.text: the whole suite self-validates in CI and goes red if any check loses its ability to catch defects
claims.evidence: conformance/ci/run_suite.py (killrate/merchant/demo-drift gates) + .github/workflows self-testing CI

claims.text: VERDICT: INCOMPLETE — 21/98 applicable MUSTs (21%), 0 deviations
claims.evidence: Illustrative sample of the real CLI output format (packaging/spck_conformance/); numbers are an example, not a product stat — mark data-example on redesign so the claims gate can distinguish it

claims.text: Python 3.9+. Published on PyPI.
claims.evidence: packaging/pyproject.toml L10 requires-python = ">=3.9"; https://pypi.org/project/spck-conformance/

claims.text: Stdlib-light, one dependency, no account required.
claims.evidence: packaging/pyproject.toml L21 dependencies = ["certifi>=2023.7.22"] — exactly one runtime dependency

claims.text: 0 clean · 2 a MUST deviated · 1 couldn't run
claims.evidence: packaging/spck_conformance/cli.py L15 exit-code contract + return paths

claims.text: it fails the build on any MUST deviation and writes a JUnit report
claims.evidence: action.yml (junit input; description states the exit-code semantics)

claims.text: 193 checks across checkout, order, discount, catalog, cart, fulfillment, totals — each proven to catch its own defects, each citing a spec clause.
claims.evidence: coverage_gate.py regex '(\d+)\+? checks across' pins 193; every MCheck carries a register citation (conformance/checks/merchant.py req_id→source mapping); bind number to site_claims.json:$.manifest.merchant_checks

claims.text: Unofficial, open, kill-rate validated.
claims.evidence: conformance/selfcheck/mutation_killrate.py + run_suite killrate gate

claims.text: Write-path checks (checkout, order, cart) run only in the CLI
claims.evidence: Instant web check scope is discovery + profile + read-only catalog (functions/ + check.html copy); write paths run only in the CLI suite — this honesty line anchors the /check depth ladder

claims.text: Run the full suite (193 kill-rate-validated checks) from your shell or CI.
claims.evidence: Gate-pinned 193 (coverage_gate.py). PAGE RETIRING (Task 11): claim must not silently migrate — /check already carries the registered equivalent (CLAIM-024)

claims.text: Reports kept for 90 days.
claims.evidence: functions/api/[[path]].js L139 expirationTtl: 7776000 (= 90 days). PAGE RETIRING: saved-reports UI leaves the public surface; KV backend untouched (admin retrieval remains). Do not re-add to public pages without re-registering

Failure-modes hero stat — conformance/agent/agent_governance.py _agent_copy_freshness() enforces len(non-None DEFECTS)=43 (verified live import 2026-07-09); NOT in agent-coverage.json; data-live bind to site_claims.json:$.manifest.agent_defects

'You can watch live' stat — public/agent-demo.json:$.cases length = 6 (verified 2026-07-09); run_suite demo-drift gate keeps each case's catching check killing its defect; recommend data-live bind to agent-demo.json cases length

'Proven to catch the bug' stat — conformance/agent/run_agent.py reference gate passes only when every agent check clean-passes AND kills its targeted defect (live-run 2026-07-09: PASS); conformance/agent/agent_coverage_lock.json

claims.text: Every test is proven to catch its own bug — it passes a known-good agent and provably fails the broken one.
claims.evidence: conformance/agent/run_agent.py reference gate (clean-pass + kill per check, defect isolation)

claims.text: Watch all six live in the demo.
claims.evidence: public/agent-demo.json has exactly 6 cases (verified 2026-07-09); must stay in sync with the demo data

claims.text: 42 checks
claims.evidence: Terminal-sample check count — gate-enforced by agent_governance.py regex 'ran \d+ ops[^;<]*;\s*(\d+) checks?'. NOTE: the '8 ops' prefix in the same line is STALE (live run 2026-07-09 prints 'ran 11 ops') and is classed REWRITE in the audit — the gate deliberately does not pin the ops count

claims.text: every check passes a correct agent and catches its bug
claims.evidence: Verbatim shape of the run_agent.py PASS line (live-verified 2026-07-09: 'PASS — every agent check clean-passes the reference agent and kills its targeted defect')

claims.text: These are real recorded runs of a correct shopping agent buying from a store that behaves badly on purpose.
claims.evidence: conformance/agent/build_demo_data.py generates public/agent-demo.json from real harness runs (its _about says so)

claims.text: Real recorded runs — not a mock-up. Each flaw is proven to be caught.
claims.evidence: build_demo_data.py + run_suite demo-drift gate: every demo case's catching check must still kill its defect on each CI run

claims.text: Every normative MUST in each pinned UCP spec version is accounted as a
claims.evidence: conformance/selfcheck/verify_register_completeness.py + conformance/coverage/coverage_gate.py; public/coverage.json _about states the same contract and every row carries check/exempt/gap status

claims.text: A check only counts when it
claims.evidence: clean-pass + 100% kill on a known-good reference: conformance/selfcheck/validate_merchant_checks.py + conformance/ci/differential.py (independent official Flower Shop sample) + conformance/SOURCES.lock.json pins

claims.evidence: conformance/coverage/exemptions.json + validate_exemptions() in coverage_gate.py (machine-checked against double-booking)
